title: Pre-computed Similarity Data
impact: 1.5-2x speedup for duplicate detection by pre-computing normalized Sets once per entity instead of creating them repeatedly during O(n²) comparisons

memoryChurn.target: Low - Sets created once and reused

tasks.description: Create PreparedEntity Interface and Helper Methods - Define a PreparedEntity interface that holds pre-computed normalized data and add helper methods to prepare entities for efficient similarity comparisons

interface.name: PreparedEntity
fields: nameLower: string - Lowercase name for comparison
observationSet: Set<string> - Set of lowercase observations
tagSet: Set<string> - Set of lowercase tags

stepByStep: Find the imports section at the top (around line 10)
After the imports and before the class, add the PreparedEntity interface with JSDoc:
* Entity data pre-processed for efficient similarity comparisons.
* Pre-computes normalized Sets once to avoid repeated creation during O(n²) comparisons.
After the constructor, add the prepareEntity method:
* Prepare an entity for efficient similarity comparisons.
* Pre-computes all normalized data to avoid repeated computation.
* Prepare multiple entities for efficient similarity comparisons.
* Use this before batch comparison operations.
acceptanceCriteria: PreparedEntity interface defined with all 5 fields
prepareEntity() method creates pre-computed data correctly
prepareEntities() creates Map of entity name to PreparedEntity
All fields are properly typed
npm run typecheck passes

tasks.description: Create Optimized calculatePreparedSimilarity Method - Add a new similarity calculation method that uses PreparedEntity to avoid repeated Set creation, and add a helper for efficient set intersection size calculation

methods: private calculatePreparedSimilarity(p1: PreparedEntity, p2: PreparedEntity): number
private setIntersectionSize(a: Set<string>, b: Set<string>): number
keyDifferences: Uses pre-computed nameLower instead of calling toLowerCase()
Uses pre-computed typeLower for type comparison
Uses pre-computed observationSet instead of creating new Set
Uses pre-computed tagSet instead of creating new Set
Uses setIntersectionSize helper to avoid creating intermediate Set

stepByStep: Find the existing calculateEntitySimilarity method (around line 32)
Add the setIntersectionSize helper method before calculateEntitySimilarity:
* Efficiently calculate intersection size of two Sets without creating a new Set.
* Iterates over the smaller set for O(min(m,n)) complexity.
private setIntersectionSize(a: Set<string>, b: Set<string>): number {
const [smaller, larger] = a.size <= b.size ? [a, b] : [b, a];
Add the calculatePreparedSimilarity method after calculateEntitySimilarity:
* Calculate similarity between two prepared entities.
* OPTIMIZED: Uses pre-computed Sets to avoid O(n) set creation per comparison.
// Type similarity (exact match) - use pre-computed lowercase
// Observation overlap (Jaccard similarity) - use pre-computed Sets
// Tag overlap (Jaccard similarity) - use pre-computed Sets
Update the JSDoc on the original calculateEntitySimilarity to note the optimized alternative:
Add note: '* NOTE: For batch comparisons, use prepareEntities() + calculatePreparedSimilarity() for better performance.'
acceptanceCriteria: calculatePreparedSimilarity() method added using pre-computed Sets
setIntersectionSize() helper method added to avoid creating intermediate Set
Original calculateEntitySimilarity() preserved for backward compatibility
JSDoc documentation added noting the optimized alternative
npm run typecheck passes

Call calculatePreparedSimilarity(prepared1, prepared2) instead of calculateEntitySimilarity(entity1, entity2)

successCriteria: PreparedEntity interface and helper methods created
calculatePreparedSimilarity() method implemented using pre-computed Sets
setIntersectionSize() helper avoids intermediate Set creation
findDuplicates() prepares entities once and uses optimized similarity
notes: This sprint optimizes the duplicate detection performance by pre-computing normalized data (lowercase strings, Sets) once per entity before the O(n²) comparison loop. The original calculateEntitySimilarity creates 4 new Sets per comparison - with 100 entities that's potentially 40,000 Set creations. By pre-computing once, we reduce this to 100 Set creations. The setIntersectionSize helper further avoids creating a third Set for the intersection calculation.
